sections.label: Section 2.2.324
sections.original: O vir fortis , atque amicus . Verum hoc saepe , Phormio ,
sections.translation: O brave and kind man ! But , Phormio , I often

sections.label: Section 2.2.325
sections.original: Vereor , ne istaec fortitudo in nervum erumpat denique .
sections.translation: dread lest this courage may end in the stocks at last . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.2.325b
sections.translation: Oh , by no means ; I ’ ve made trial , and have already pondered on the paths for my feet . How many men before to-day do you suppose I have beaten , even to death , strangers as well as citizens : the better I understand it , the oftener I try it . Just tell me , look you , did you ever hear of an action of damages being brought against me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.330
sections.translation: How is that ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.330b
sections.original: Quia non rete accipitri tenditur neque miluo ,
sections.translation: Because the net is never spread for the hawk or the kite , that do us the mischief ; it is spread for those that do us none : because in the last there is profit , while with the others it is labor lost . For persons , out of whom any thing can be got , there ’ s risk from others ; they know that I ’ ve got nothing . You will say : They will take you , [*] when sentenced , into their house ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.335
sections.original: Alere nolunt hominem edacem ; et sapiunt , mea quidem sententia ,
sections.translation: they have no wish to maintain a devouring fellow ; and , in my opinion , they are wise , if for an injury they are unwilling to return the highest benefits .

sections.label: Section 2.2.337
sections.original: Non potest satis pro merito ab illo tibi referri gratia .
sections.translation: It ’ s impossible that sufficient thanks can be returned you by him for your kindness .

sections.label: Section 2.2.338
sections.original: Immo enim nemo satis pro merito gratiam regi refert .
sections.translation: Why no ; no person can return thanks sufficient to his patron [*] for his kindness . For you to take your place at table at free cost , [*] anointed and just washed at the bath ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.340
sections.original: Otiosum ab animo , quum ille et cura et sumtu absumitur
sections.translation: with your mind at ease , whereas he is devoured with the care and expense : while every thing is being done to give you delight , he is being vexed at heart ; you are laughing away , first to drink , [*] take the higher place ; a banquet full of doubts [*] is placed before you —

sections.label: Section 2.2.343
sections.translation: What is the meaning of that expression ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.343b
sections.original: Ubi tu dubites quid sumas potissimum .
sections.translation: When you are in doubt which in especial to par-take of . When you enter upon a consideration how delicious these things are , and how costly they are ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.345
sections.original: Ea qui praebet non tu hunc habeas plane praesentem Deum ?
sections.translation: the person who provides them , must you not account him a very God — neither more nor less ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.346
sections.original: Senex adest : vide quid agas : prima coitio est acerrima .
sections.translation: The old man is coming ; take care what you are about ; the first onset is the fiercest ; if you stand that , then , afterward , you may play just as you please .

sections.label: Section 2.3.348
sections.translation: Well now — did you ever hear of an injury being done to any person in a more affronting manner than this has to me ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.350
sections.translation: Assist me , I do beg of you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.350b
sections.translation: He ’ s in a passion .

sections.label: Section 2.3.350c
sections.translation: Do you mind your cue ; I ’ ll rouse him just now . ( Stepping forward and crying aloud .) Oh immortal Gods ! does Demipho deny that Phanium here is related to him ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.353b
sections.translation: He does deny it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.354
sections.original: Neque eius patrem se scire qui fuerit ?
sections.translation: I believe it is the very man I was speaking about . Follow me . ( They all come forward .)

sections.label: Section 2.3.354b
sections.translation: And that he knows who her father was ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.355
sections.original: Ipsum esse opinor de quo agebam . Sequimini .
sections.translation: He does deny it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.356
sections.original: Nec Stilphonem ipsum scire qui fuerit ?
sections.translation: And that he knows who Stilpho was ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.356b
sections.translation: He does deny it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.357
sections.original: Quia egens relicta est misera , ignoratur parens ,
sections.translation: Because the poor thing was left destitute , her father is disowned ; she herself is slighted : see what avarice does .

sections.label: Section 2.3.359
sections.original: Si herum insimulabis avaritiae , male audies .
sections.translation: If you are going to accuse my : master of avarice , you shall hear what you won ’ t like .

sections.label: Section 2.3.360
sections.original: O audaciam ! etiam me ultro accusatum advenit ?
sections.translation: Oh , the impudence of the fellow ! Does he come on purpose to accuse me ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.361
sections.original: Nam iam adolescenti nihil est quod succenseam ,
sections.translation: For really , I have no reason why I should be offended at the young man , if he did not know him ; since that person , when growing aged and poor , and supporting himself by his labor , generally confined himself to the country ; there he had a piece of land from my father

sections.label: Section 2.3.365
sections.original: Colendum habebat . Saepe interea mihi senex
sections.translation: to cultivate ; full oft , in the mean time , did the old . man tell me that this kinsman of his neglected him : but what a man ? The very best I ever saw in all my life .

sections.label: Section 2.3.368
sections.original: Videas te atque illum ut narras .
sections.translation: Look to yourself as well as to him , how you speak .

sections.label: Section 2.3.368b
sections.translation: Away , to utter perdition , with you . For if I had not formed such an opinion of him , I should never

sections.label: Section 2.3.370
sections.original: Ob hanc inimicitias caperem in vestram familiam ,
sections.translation: have incurred such enmity with your family on her account , whom he now slights in such an ungenerous manner .

sections.label: Section 2.3.372
sections.original: Pergin hero absenti male loqui , impurissime ?
sections.translation: What , do you persist in speaking abusively of my master in his absence , you most abominable fellow ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.373
sections.translation: Why , it ’ s just what he deserves .

sections.label: Section 2.3.373b
sections.translation: Say you so , you jail-bird ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.373c
sections.translation: Geta !

sections.label: Section 2.3.374
sections.translation: A plunderer of people ’ s property — a perverter of the laws !

sections.label: Section 2.3.374b
sections.translation: Geta !

sections.label: Section 2.3.375
sections.translation: Answer him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.375b
sections.translation: Who is it ? ( Looking round .) Oh !—

sections.label: Section 2.3.375c
sections.translation: Hold your peace .

sections.label: Section 2.3.375d
sections.translation: He has never left off uttering abuse against you behind your back , unworthy of you , and just befitting himself .

sections.label: Section 2.3.377b
sections.translation: Well now , have done . ( Addressing PHORMIO .) Young man , in the first place , with your good leave , I ask you this , if you may possibly be pleased to give me an answer :

sections.label: Section 2.3.380
sections.original: Quem amicum tuum ais fuisse istum ? explana mihi :
sections.translation: explain to me who this friend of yours was , that you speak of , and how he said that he was related to me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.382
sections.translation: You are fishing it out , just as if you didn ’ t know .

sections.label: Section 2.3.382b
sections.translation: I , know ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.382c
sections.translation: Yes .

sections.label: Section 2.3.383
sections.original: Ego me nego : tu qui ais redige in memoriam .
sections.translation: I say I do not ; you , Who affirm it , recall it to my recollection .

sections.label: Section 2.3.384
sections.original: Eho , tu sobrinum tuum non noras ?
sections.translation: Come now , didn ’ t you know your own cousin-ger-man ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.384b
sections.translation: You torture me to death ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.385
sections.translation: tell me his name .

sections.label: Section 2.3.385b
sections.translation: His name ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.385c
sections.translation: Of course . ( PHORMIO hesitates .) Why are you silent now ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.386
sections.translation: Heavens , I ’ m undone ; I ’ ve forgot the name .

sections.label: Section 2.3.386b
sections.translation: Well , what do you say ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.386c
sections.translation: Geta , if you recollect the name I told you a short time since , prompt me . ( Aloud , to DEMIPHO .) Well then , I sha ’ n ’ t tell you ; as if you didn ’ t know , you come to pump me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.389
sections.translation: I , come to pump you , indeed ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.389b
sections.translation: Stilpho .

sections.label: Section 2.3.389c
sections.translation: But , after all , what matters that to me ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.390
sections.translation: It is Stilpho .

sections.label: Section 2.3.390b
sections.translation: Whom did you say ?
